name: sand_cube_190x210_rear_corner_sweeps_d_squat_absorber
status: 40 mm rear-corner floor sweeps with exact rear-flush squat absorber and unchanged serviceable exterior tower

wall_stack_geometry.method: concentric all-edge filleted envelopes clipped above the solid floor

enclosure.construction: The established 190 x 210 x 190 enclosure, centered black-hole face, GX16, fill blisters, solid floor, 2-3-2 walls, and port-relieved brace network are retained. Only the upper assembly changes: its local roof gap is filled into a solid 7 mm insert island, and the existing top rail flows into a constant-width D-shaped cradle around the hidden flange.

scaled_black_hole.design_intent: The woofer remains dead-centered and the contour extends exactly to the cabinet edge-radius tangent on all four sides. Its 21.75 mm radial blend is substantially gentler than the previous 161 mm-diameter draft while preserving the larger design's exact axial depth.

internal_bracing.design_intent: The original equal-height top and side brace network and all four conformal black-hole roots remain. Near the roof cartridge, the top rail flows into a smooth constant-width D-shaped cradle. Its inner and outer boundaries follow the flange and roof-island curvature, while a full-height installation keepout prevents any front or rear overhang.

alignment.type: single 40 mm circular port with an R75 lower-left rear sweep, R50 lower-right rear rise, exact rear-flush 56 mm absorber straight, four rotated R35.7 upper elbows, and unchanged exterior tower

dsp_assisted_goal.design_goal: No audible 39-40 Hz attenuation at moderate playback with DSP
dsp_assisted_goal.moderate_volume_definition: Use the bass limiter so boosted low-frequency demand remains near the 25-50 W acoustic cases; do not treat the TPA3255 voltage capability as permission to run 200 W of continuous boosted bass.
dsp_assisted_goal.required_protection: 4th-order high-pass at 28-30 Hz plus dynamic bass limiting
dsp_assisted_goal.qualification: The DSP case is evaluated at 39 Hz below the natural vent tuning. Final audibility depends on room gain, placement, leakage, and the measured unit.

volume_accounting.method: Exact OpenCascade intersection of the printed cavity features, supplied woofer solids, and complete in-box port envelope with the acoustic domain, followed by guarded pairwise inclusion-exclusion. The detailed GX16 STEP uses its conservative in-cavity bounding-box intersection because its surface model makes an exact boolean disproportionately slow. Binding posts and amplifier electronics are deferred.

internally_mounted_upper_tower.visible_profile: straight 40 mm bore / 50 mm OD circle

port.header_manufacturing_analogy: constant-radius elbows and short straight coupons, with elbow planes rotated in 3D like a fabricated exhaust header

response_and_velocity.model: Small-signal electro-mechano-acoustic lumped model using Dayton's series-coil T/S parameters, the locally modeled net volume and airway area, and port loss Q=10.
response_and_velocity.recommended_protection: Use a 4th-order high-pass at 28-30 Hz and dynamic bass limiting.

horn_and_de250.requested_wording_checked: No BMC component exists; the supplied and fitted component is B&C DE250.
horn_and_de250.driver_mount_status: reference placement only; tube-to-DE250 structure deferred

moderate_volume_limits: The 40 mm bore has 3.9% more area than the smooth 39.243 mm variant, lowering velocity for the same volume flow but requiring more effective length at the same box volume and tuning.
The floor route uses broad R75 and R50 corner sweeps. The four upper steering elbows use R35.7 centerlines (0.893D), retain the 40 mm circular bore, and avoid spline or flattened sections.
No empirical bend-loss correction is baked into the Helmholtz length. Final tuning, compression, and noise must be measured on a printed core before mounts and couplers are frozen.

serviceable_tower.installation_direction: tower cartridge inserted from inside through roof

top_brace.retention: three underside screws into blind solid-roof inserts

deferred: snap features for the removable flare
external tube ribs or constrained-layer damping
